text.title: Pricing Intelligence
text.summary: Triangulating list price against realized ACV, packaging diff detection, and AB-test interference rules.

rule.statement: Triangulate competitor pricing three ways: posted list price, G2 reviewer-disclosed spend, and ex-customer or buyer interview. Publish the median and the spread — not a single number.
rule.rationale: B2B SaaS discounting averages 15-35% off list price. Published list price is aspirational; realized ACV from buyer disclosures is the actual competitive benchmark. Single-source pricing intel is misleading.

list.item: 1st — Ex-customer or buyer interview: actual invoiced amount, contract length, included seats/usage
2nd — Vendr/Tropic realized-price indexes: negotiated benchmarks from procurement platforms
3rd — G2 reviewer-disclosed spend: reviewer-reported price band with contract renewal status
4th — Pricing page scrape: list price, requires 3+ independent fetches over 14 days to confirm
5th — Press releases or analyst reports: directional only; cite as "secondary"

rule.statement: For marketplaces, always compute all-in take rate: commission + payment processing + seller subscription + ad platform fees. Never report headline commission alone.
rule.rationale: Headline commission (e.g., Etsy 6.5%) understates the real take by 2x or more when seller subscriptions, payment fees, and ad revenue are included. All-in rate is what sellers actually experience.

rule.statement: Require at least 3 independent fetches of a competitor pricing page over 14 days before declaring a pricing change. Single-fetch divergence is likely AB testing, not a price change.
rule.rationale: Competitors run pricing AB tests continuously; a daily fetch sees noise. Premature "competitor dropped prices" signals based on one fetch trigger incorrect strategic responses.

section.title: Packaging Schema
section.p: Normalize all competitor pricing to a canonical schema: tier name, included seats or usage quota, overage rate, add-on price, contract length options, billing period (monthly/annual), currency, and source URL with retrieval date. Without normalization, cross-competitor comparisons mix incompatible denominators.

rule.statement: Pin currency, billing period, unit (per-seat / per-org / per-usage), and contract length in the pricing schema. "$10/month" without these fields cannot be compared across competitors.
rule.rationale: Currency and term ambiguity is the number-one cause of pricing benchmark errors. A per-seat monthly price cannot be added to a per-org annual price without normalization.
